name: Costa Rica (state flag)

colors.symbolism: the sky, opportunities, intellectual thought, perseverance and the eternity of God
clear thinking, happiness, wisdom, power, the beauty of the sky and peace
the warmth and generosity of the people, the blood spilt for freedom and the bravery of the nation
elements of the national coat of arms
the volcanoes and the central mountain range in the coat of arms

description: Five horizontal stripes in 1:1:2:1:1 proportion — blue, white, red, white, blue — with the national coat of arms of Costa Rica placed in a white oval positioned toward the hoist on the central red stripe.
history: Designed by Pacífica Fernández, wife of President José María Castro Madriz, the pentaband layout was adopted on 29 September 1848 with colours inspired by the French Tricolour. The state flag is distinguished from the civil flag by the inclusion of the national coat of arms in a white oval offset toward the hoist on the red stripe. The current coat of arms — depicting three smoking volcanoes representing the central, Guanacaste and Talamanca ranges, two oceans with sailing ships, and seven stars for the country's seven provinces — was set in its present form by Law No. 7,839 of 21 September 1998.

features.symbolism: blue for the sky and ideals; white for clear thinking and peace; red — the central double-width stripe — for the warmth and generosity of Costa Ricans and the blood spilt for freedom

features.role: national coat of arms (within a white oval on the central red stripe, offset toward the hoist)
features.symbolism: the coat of arms in a white oval near the hoist depicts three smoking volcanoes (the Central, Guanacaste and Talamanca cordilleras), two oceans with sailing ships representing the country's coasts on the Caribbean and the Pacific, a rising sun, and seven gold stars for Costa Rica's seven provinces; the current form was set by Law 7,839 of 1998
